missingContent.suggestedAddition: Add a section explaining that JIT provisioning can be enabled to automatically create Drata user accounts on first SSO login, removing the need to pre-invite each user.

missingContent.githubEvidence: enforcedDomains: string[] — allows requiring SSO for specific email domains
missingContent.suggestedAddition: Document the domain enforcement option that forces all users with matching email domains to authenticate via SSO only.

discrepancies.articleSays: Navigate to Settings > Security > Single Sign-On
discrepancies.githubSays: The 'Security' tab was renamed to 'Authentication' in a recent release. The correct menu path is now Settings > Authentication > Single Sign-On.
discrepancies.explanation: Users following the article will not find the SSO settings page because the menu label changed.

discrepancies.articleSays: Drata supports Okta, OneLogin, and Azure AD
discrepancies.githubSays: Three additional SSO providers have been added since this article was written: Google Workspace, JumpCloud, and PingIdentity.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Drata supports the following SSO providers: Okta, OneLogin, Azure AD, Google Workspace, JumpCloud, and PingIdentity.
discrepancies.explanation: Customers using Google Workspace, JumpCloud, or PingIdentity may not realize Drata supports their provider.

thirdPartyIssues.context: Drata supports Okta, OneLogin, and Azure Active Directory for SSO.
thirdPartyIssues.suggestedFix: Replace 'Azure Active Directory' with 'Microsoft Entra ID' throughout the article. Microsoft rebranded Azure AD to Entra ID in 2023.
thirdPartyIssues.explanation: Microsoft rebranded Azure Active Directory to Microsoft Entra ID in July 2023; the old name is still understood but will confuse users searching for the product.

missingContent.suggestedAddition: Document that individual integrations can be paused and resumed without disabling the entire connection, useful during maintenance windows.

discrepancies.articleSays: Evidence can be collected daily, weekly, or monthly
discrepancies.githubSays: Two new collection frequencies were added: hourly (for high-frequency monitoring) and quarterly (for periodic compliance cycles).
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Evidence can be collected on the following schedules: hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly. Select the frequency that best matches your compliance requirements.
discrepancies.explanation: Compliance teams looking for hourly or quarterly collection won't know it's available.

articles.title: Connecting your AWS account to Drata

discrepancies.githubSays: Drata no longer uses AWS's broad ReadOnlyAccess policy. It now requires a custom least-privilege policy with 22 specific permissions for security compliance reasons.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Instead of attaching the ReadOnlyAccess managed policy, create and attach Drata's custom IAM policy. Download the policy JSON from the Connections setup page in Drata, then create a new IAM policy in AWS using that file.
discrepancies.explanation: Customers following the old instructions will grant overly broad AWS access, which is a security risk and may fail Drata's own permission validation.

discrepancies.githubSays: The required stack name changed to 'drata-aws-connector'. Using the old name will cause the setup to fail.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: When prompted for a stack name, enter **drata-aws-connector** (do not change this value).
discrepancies.explanation: Wrong stack name will cause the integration setup to fail with no clear error message.

thirdPartyIssues.context: In the AWS Management Console, navigate to Services > CloudFormation to create the stack.
thirdPartyIssues.currentTerm: AWS Console search bar or direct URL — the top-level Services menu was removed in the AWS Console redesign.
thirdPartyIssues.suggestedFix: In the AWS Console, search for 'CloudFormation' in the search bar at the top, or navigate directly to console.aws.amazon.com/cloudformation.
thirdPartyIssues.explanation: AWS redesigned the console navigation in 2023, removing the top-level 'Services' dropdown menu in favor of a unified search bar.

thirdPartyIssues.context: In IAM, attach the ReadOnlyAccess managed policy to the Drata role.
thirdPartyIssues.currentTerm: Custom least-privilege IAM policy (also flagged as a discrepancy from GitHub)
thirdPartyIssues.suggestedFix: Do not use the AWS-managed ReadOnlyAccess policy. Instead, use Drata's custom IAM policy JSON available on the Connections setup page.
thirdPartyIssues.explanation: Drata migrated away from ReadOnlyAccess to a custom least-privilege policy for security compliance reasons.

articles.title: Inviting team members and managing roles

discrepancies.articleSays: Users can be assigned: Administrator, Auditor, or Viewer
discrepancies.githubSays: Two new roles have been added — Owner and Reviewer — and the Viewer role has been renamed to Read Only in new accounts.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Users can be assigned one of the following roles: Owner, Administrator, Auditor, Reviewer, or Read Only. The Owner role has full account control; Read Only provides view-only access.
discrepancies.explanation: Article is missing the Owner and Reviewer roles and uses the deprecated Viewer role name.

articles.title: Connecting GitHub for code repository monitoring

discrepancies.githubSays: A fourth OAuth scope was added — read:audit_log — to support the new audit trail feature. Connections authorized before this change will need to be reauthorized.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Drata requires the following OAuth scopes: repo, read:org, read:user, and read:audit_log. If you connected GitHub before June 2024, reauthorize the connection to grant the new scope.
discrepancies.explanation: Missing scope will cause audit trail features to fail for users who authorized before the scope was added.

articles.title: HIPAA compliance checklist for healthcare organizations
Setting up Slack notifications for compliance alerts

thirdPartyIssues.context: To install the Drata app, visit the Slack App Directory and search for 'Drata'.
thirdPartyIssues.suggestedFix: To install the Drata app, visit the Slack Marketplace (slack.com/marketplace) and search for 'Drata'.
thirdPartyIssues.explanation: Slack rebranded the App Directory to Slack Marketplace in 2024; the old name still redirects but the UI label no longer matches.

thirdPartyIssues.context: Navigate to Workspace Settings > Notifications > Apps to manage which apps can post to channels.
thirdPartyIssues.suggestedFix: In Slack, open the menu next to your workspace name and go to Settings & administration > Manage apps to control which apps can post to channels.
thirdPartyIssues.explanation: Slack reorganized its admin navigation in the 2023 redesign; the old Workspace Settings > Notifications > Apps path no longer exists.

discrepancies.articleSays: Vendors are scored Low, Medium, or High based on their questionnaire responses
discrepancies.githubSays: The risk scoring system expanded from 3 tiers to 5. The new tiers are: Critical, High, Medium, Low, and Minimal.
discrepancies.suggestedFix: Vendors are assigned one of five risk tiers based on their questionnaire responses: Critical, High, Medium, Low, or Minimal. Critical vendors require immediate remediation; Minimal vendors pose negligible risk.
discrepancies.explanation: Article uses old 3-tier system; users won't understand why their vendor shows 'Critical' or 'Minimal' risk.
